behavior_summary: U8P_STMITE::look is a simple examine handler that displays the short descriptive bark "stalagmite " to the player and then suspends the U8P_STMITE::look process.

U8P_STMITE::func00AE is a helper routine that runs a short frame-based visual/placement animation on the item instance: it iterates a frame index, sets the item's frame, updates scene stacking (push/pop) with a small Z offset derived from the current frame, spawns a short-lived helper process each iteration, suspends between iterations, and finally resets the item's type and frame.
The helper operates on the implicit item instance (this). It uses two temporary locals (local1 as an iteration counter and local2 as a small Z-offset/bitmask derived from the current frame).

Several engine Item::* APIs are used for visual manipulation (Item::setFrame, Item::push, Item::getFrame, Item::pop, Item::getZ/getY/getX, Item::setType) and spawn is used to start FREE::func1C19 for transient work.
notable_control_flow: U8P_STMITE::look: immediate bark of text "stalagmite " then suspend — no branching or external calls.

U8P_STMITE::func00AE: initialize local1=1, local2=0; loop while local1 < 12: call Item::setFrame(local1,this), Item::push(this), read Item::getFrame(this) and map certain frame values to small offsets (local2), call Item::pop(getZ(this)+local2, getY(this), getX(this), this), spawn FREE::func1C19(0), suspend, increment local1; after loop call Item::setType(0x0305,this) and Item::setFrame(0,this).
Frame->offset mapping is frame values in the range (0x05..0x0A) mapped to small offsets (examples: 0x05->0x01, 0x06->0x02, 0x07->0x04, 0x08->0x04, 0x09->0x02, 0x0A->0x04).
open_questions: The exact behavior and purpose of spawned FREE::func1C19(0) are not present in this script and remain unknown (likely a transient visual effect or cleanup).
Precise timing and visual effect perceived by players (e.g., how long each suspend yields, and whether these appear as a smooth animation) is inferred but not explicit.
Reason for setting item type to 0x0305 at the end: inferred to be a final state change but the gameplay semantics of that type id in the wider game are not shown here.

main_function.name: U8P_STMITE::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the player-facing short description when the item is examined (bark "stalagmite ") and then suspend/end the U8P_STMITE::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player performs the 'U8P_STMITE::look' / examine action on this item (engine triggers the object's U8P_STMITE::look handler).
Runs as the object's U8P_STMITE::look/inspection process (processType 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_STMITE::look
function_map.role: examine/U8P_STMITE::look handler that emits a short description

function_map.name: U8P_STMITE::func00AE
function_map.role: frame-based visual/placement animation loop operating on the item instance (implicit this)

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: engine API to set visible item frame

function_map.name: Item::push
function_map.role: engine API to push/update scene/stack state for an item

function_map.name: Item::pop
function_map.role: engine API to pop/remove item at a location (used here with Z offset)

function_map.name: spawn ->FREE::func1C19
function_map.role: spawn of a short-lived/free helper process for transient effect or cleanup

external_calls.purpose: Change the item's visible animation/frame index each iteration and finally reset frame to 0.
Update scene/stack state before computing offsets/pop placement (prepares visual state).
Read current frame in order to map certain frame values to a small Z offset (drives local2).
Read base Z coordinate to compute pop Z = getZ + local2.
Provide Y coordinate for Item::pop placement.
Provide X coordinate for Item::pop placement.
Reposition/pop the item at computed coordinates (Z+local2, Y, X) to create the stepped visual placement.
Trigger a short-lived/free helper process each iteration, likely for a transient visual effect or cleanup; spawn return/pid is not used further here.
Set the item's type to 0x0305 at the end of the helper routine as a final state change.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_STMITE::look is a simple examine handler that displays the short descriptive bark "stalagmite " to the player and then suspends the U8P_STMITE::look process.
